zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond於1245年出生在英國肯特托布利其時，他的父親Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester23歲，母親Maud de Lacy22歲。他於1275年2月與Lady Juliane FitzMaurice在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1287年8月29日在愛爾蘭康瑙特享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond于1245年出生在英国肯特托布利其时，他的父亲Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester23岁，母亲Maud de Lacy22岁。他于1275年2月与Lady Juliane FitzMaurice在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1287年8月29日在爱尔兰康瑙特享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond was born in 1245, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, his father, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, was 23 and his mother, Maud de Lacy, was 22. He married Lady Juliane FitzMaurice in February 1275, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 29 August 1287, in Connaught, Ireland, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond est né en 1245, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Maud de Lacy, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Juliane FitzMaurice en février 1275, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 29 août 1287, à Connaught, Irlande, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond 1245 in Tonbridge, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester 23 und seine Mutter Maud de Lacy 22. Er hat im Februar 1275 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Lady Juliane FitzMaurice geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 29. August 1287 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Connaught, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond è nato il nel 1245 nel Tonbridge, Kent, England, il suo padre, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Lacy, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Lady Juliane FitzMaurice nel febbraio 1275, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 agosto 1287, nel Connaught, Irlanda, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomondが1245年にイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucesterは23歳，母Maud de Lacyは22歳であった。1275年2月にイギリスイギリスエセックスでLady Juliane FitzMauriceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1287年8月29日にアイルランドコナウトで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond(이)가 1245년 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Lacy(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1275년 2월 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Lady Juliane FitzMaurice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1287년 8월 29일 아일랜드 콘오그h트에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond nasceu em 1245, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Lacy, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Juliane FitzMaurice em fevereiro de 1275, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de agosto de 1287, em Connaught, Irlanda, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond родился в 1245 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Lacy, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Juliane FitzMaurice февраль 1275 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 29 августа 1287 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas de Clare 1st Lord of Thomond nació en 1245, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Maud de Lacy, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Lady Juliane FitzMaurice en febrero de 1275, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 29 de agosto de 1287, en Connaught, Irlanda, a la edad de 42 años.
